agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"E-commerce platform managing the product catalog, shopping cart, checkout, promotions, order lifecycle, and refund processes. The domain encodes inventory reservation semantics, promotional pricing exclusivity, order immutability post-payment, and time-bound refund eligibility.","bounded_context":"Order Management & Checkout — covers cart operations, inventory reservation, checkout flow, discount/promotion application, payment confirmation, and post-purchase refund policy.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Cart","definition":"A transient collection of items a customer intends to purchase. Adding items to the cart does NOT reserve inventory."},{"term":"Inventory Reservation","definition":"A time-limited hold on inventory units that begins when checkout is initiated, preventing other customers from purchasing those units. Released if payment is not completed within the reservation window."},{"term":"Reservation Window","definition":"The 15-minute period starting when checkout begins during which inventory is held for the customer. Expiry triggers automatic release."},{"term":"Checkout","definition":"The process initiated by a customer to finalize a purchase. Triggers inventory reservation and starts the reservation window."},{"term":"Abandoned Checkout","definition":"A checkout session where payment is not completed within the 15-minute reservation window."},{"term":"Active Promotion","definition":"A currently effective promotional pricing rule applied to a product. A product may have at most one active promotion at any time."},{"term":"Discount Code","definition":"A customer-supplied code that applies a discount to an order. Mutually exclusive with promotional pricing — only one discount mechanism may apply per order."},{"term":"Discount Mechanism","definition":"Any source of price reduction: either a discount code OR a product's active promotion. Exactly one may apply per order."},{"term":"Payment Confirmed","definition":"The terminal payment state after which the order becomes immutable. No modifications are permitted."},{"term":"Order Immutability","definition":"The constraint that an order's contents, pricing, and metadata cannot be changed after payment is confirmed."},{"term":"Zero Inventory","definition":"A product state where available stock is zero. Such products are unpurchasable regardless of catalog listing status."},{"term":"Refund Window","definition":"The 30-day period after delivery during which a customer may request a refund."},{"term":"Delivery Date","definition":"The date on which an order is marked as delivered, which starts the refund window clock."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"Adding items to the cart does not reserve inventory. Inventory is only reserved when the checkout process begins.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"If inventory were reserved at cart-add time, phantom holds would corrupt available stock counts across the system. The cart-does-not-reserve semantic is a data-consistency constraint.","testable_assertion":"After a customer adds an item to their cart, the product's available inventory count remains unchanged."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"If checkout is abandoned (no payment within 15 minutes of checkout initiation), the inventory reservation must be released.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Failure to release reservations on timeout would permanently leak inventory, corrupting stock availability — a state integrity issue.","testable_assertion":"When 15 minutes elapse after checkout begins without payment confirmation, the reserved inventory units are returned to available stock."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"A product can have a maximum of one active promotion at a time.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Multiple active promotions on a single product create an ambiguous price, corrupting the pricing model. This is a data consistency constraint — the system cannot deterministically resolve the product's promotional price with multiple active promotions.","testable_assertion":"Attempting to activate a second promotion on a product that already has an active promotion must fail or deactivate the existing one first."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Discount codes cannot be combined with promotional pricing. Only one discount mechanism applies per order.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Allowing both would produce incorrect pricing that violates the business's pricing integrity. The system must have a single unambiguous price derivation path.","testable_assertion":"When a product in the order has an active promotion and the customer applies a discount code, only one discount mechanism is applied to the order total; the system must not stack both."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Orders cannot be modified after payment is confirmed.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Post-payment modification would break financial reconciliation, audit trails, and downstream fulfillment processes. This is a state integrity constraint — a confirmed order is an immutable financial record.","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to change order contents, quantities, or pricing after payment confirmation must be rejected by the system."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"A product with zero inventory cannot be purchased, even if it is still listed in the catalog.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Selling nonexistent stock creates fulfillment obligations that cannot be met — a physical constraint. The system would enter a corrupt state by accepting money for undeliverable goods.","testable_assertion":"When a customer attempts to check out a product whose available inventory is zero, the checkout must fail with an appropriate error."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"Refunds must be requested within 30 days of the delivery date.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 30-day window is a business policy that could be changed by management (e.g., extended to 60 days for holidays). It does not represent a physical or data-integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"A refund request submitted more than 30 days after the recorded delivery date must be rejected."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A customer has items in their cart","when":"The customer has not yet initiated checkout","then":"The product's available inventory count is not reduced and other customers can still purchase those items"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A customer initiates checkout with items in their cart","when":"Checkout begins","then":"Inventory for each item in the cart is reserved, reducing available stock by the quantities in the cart"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A customer has initiated checkout and inventory is reserved","when":"15 minutes elapse without payment confirmation","then":"All inventory reservations for that checkout session are released and available stock is restored"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A customer has initiated checkout and inventory is reserved","when":"Payment is confirmed within 15 minutes","then":"The reservation is converted to a confirmed sale and inventory is permanently decremented"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A product already has an active promotion","when":"An attempt is made to add a second promotion to the same product","then":"The system rejects the second promotion (or requires deactivation of the first), ensuring at most one active promotion exists"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A customer's order contains a product with an active promotion","when":"The customer applies a discount code to the order","then":"Only one discount mechanism is applied — the system either applies the discount code OR the promotional pricing, not both"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"An order has been paid and payment is confirmed","when":"Any attempt is made to modify the order (change items, quantities, shipping, pricing)","then":"The modification is rejected and the order remains unchanged"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A product is listed in the catalog but has zero available inventory","when":"A customer attempts to add it to cart and proceed to checkout","then":"The system prevents the purchase, displaying an out-of-stock error at or before checkout"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"An order has been delivered and the delivery date is recorded","when":"A customer requests a refund within 30 days of the delivery date","then":"The refund request is accepted for processing"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"An order was delivered more than 30 days ago","when":"A customer requests a refund","then":"The refund request is rejected with a message indicating the refund window has expired"},{"id":"AC-11","given":"Multiple customers are viewing the same product with limited inventory","when":"Customer A initiates checkout reserving the last unit, then Customer B initiates checkout for the same product","then":"Customer B's checkout fails because available inventory is zero after Customer A's reservation"},{"id":"AC-12","given":"A customer initiates checkout and inventory is reserved","when":"The checkout is abandoned and the reservation expires, then another customer initiates checkout for the same product","then":"The second customer successfully reserves the now-available inventory"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Customer adds last unit to cart, another customer checks out that unit before the first customer initiates checkout","expected_behavior":"First customer's checkout attempt fails with an out-of-stock error since cart does not reserve inventory and the unit was sold to the second customer."},{"scenario":"Reservation timer expires at the exact moment payment confirmation is received","expected_behavior":"The system must handle this race condition atomically. Either the payment succeeds (reservation honored) or fails (reservation already released). Partial states must not occur."},{"scenario":"A product's promotion is deactivated mid-checkout after the customer has seen the promotional price","expected_behavior":"The price locked at checkout initiation (when reservation began) should be honored for that session. Alternatively, the system must define whether price is locked at cart-add or checkout-begin."},{"scenario":"Customer applies a discount code to an order where one product has a promotion and another does not","expected_behavior":"The discount exclusivity rule must be clarified: does it apply per line item or per order? If per order, the discount code cannot be used at all when any item has a promotional price."},{"scenario":"Inventory drops to zero between cart-add and checkout-begin due to other purchases","expected_behavior":"Checkout initiation must validate current inventory levels and fail if insufficient stock exists, since the cart did not reserve anything."},{"scenario":"Customer requests refund exactly on the 30th day after delivery","expected_behavior":"The refund request should be accepted — the 30-day window is inclusive of the 30th day (boundary condition must be explicitly defined)."},{"scenario":"An order contains multiple items, some with promotions and some without, and customer applies a discount code","expected_behavior":"System must resolve which discount mechanism applies. The rule says one discount mechanism per order, so either the discount code applies (ignoring all promotions) or promotional pricing applies (rejecting the code)."},{"scenario":"Product inventory reaches zero after checkout reservation but before payment","expected_behavior":"The reservation protects the customer — payment should still succeed since those units are reserved for this checkout session."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Incorrect inventory reservation logic could lead to overselling (fulfillment costs, compensation) or underselling (lost revenue from phantom holds). Discount stacking bugs directly erode margins. Refund window enforcement protects against late-return financial exposure.","operational":"The 15-minute reservation window balances conversion opportunity against inventory lock-up. Too short loses sales; too long starves other customers. Order immutability post-payment simplifies fulfillment pipeline and audit compliance.","user":"Customers expect cart contents to remain available through checkout — failed checkouts due to inventory gaps create frustration. Clear discount application rules prevent pricing confusion. The 30-day refund window sets transparent expectations.","risk":"Race conditions at reservation boundaries (timer expiry vs. payment) pose the highest technical risk. Discount mechanism ambiguity (per-order vs. per-line-item) could result in incorrect pricing if not clarified. Zero-inventory bypass would create undeliverable orders.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Experience checkout failures if inventory reservation is unreliable; may see confusing pricing if discount rules are inconsistently applied; rely on 30-day refund window for purchase confidence."},{"group":"Warehouse / Fulfillment","impact":"Depends on accurate inventory counts — overselling creates pick-and-ship failures and backorder overhead."},{"group":"Finance / Accounting","impact":"Order immutability post-payment is critical for reconciliation. Discount stacking bugs cause revenue leakage. Refund window enforcement affects cash flow forecasting."},{"group":"Marketing / Merchandising","impact":"Promotion exclusivity (one per product) constrains campaign design. Discount code vs. promotion mutual exclusivity must be communicated clearly in campaigns."},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Handles edge cases around abandoned checkouts, refund window disputes, and discount application complaints. Clear rules reduce support volume."}]},"confidence":{"level":72,"reasoning":"The spec is well-articulated with seven clear business rules that are internally consistent. No contradictions detected. Confidence is below 80 because several operational details are unspecified: discount mechanism resolution priority, per-order vs per-line-item discount exclusivity, price-lock timing, and boundary condition definitions. These are not escalation-level gaps but reduce confidence in full implementation correctness.","high_confidence_areas":["Inventory reservation lifecycle (cart → checkout → payment/expiry)","Order immutability after payment confirmation","One active promotion per product constraint","Zero inventory blocks purchase","30-day refund window from delivery date"],"low_confidence_areas":["Discount mechanism priority: when both a discount code and promotional pricing are applicable, which one wins? The spec says only one applies but does not specify the resolution rule.","Discount exclusivity scope: does the one-discount-mechanism rule apply per order or per line item? An order with mixed promotional/non-promotional items is ambiguous.","Price lock timing: at what point in the flow is the price locked for the customer — cart-add, checkout-begin, or payment confirmation?","Refund window boundary: is the 30th day inclusive or exclusive?","Concurrent reservation handling: specific behavior when multiple customers race for limited inventory at checkout initiation"],"assumptions":["The 15-minute reservation window is a hard cutoff, not extendable","Inventory counts are managed as available stock (total minus reserved minus sold)","The refund window of 30 days is calendar days, not business days","Payment confirmation is a discrete, atomic event — not a multi-step process","Products listed with zero inventory are still browsable but not purchasable"]},"escalations":[],"rejection_reasons":[]}

The domain encodes inventory reservation semantics, promotional pricing exclusivity, order immutability post-payment, and time-bound refund eligibility.","bounded_context":"Order Management & Checkout — covers cart operations, inventory reservation, checkout flow, discount/promotion application, payment confirmation, and post-purcha

content.thinking:
Let me analyze this e-commerce domain scenario carefully.

The rules are clear and well-defined. Let me check for any issues:

1. Cart doesn't reserve inventory - clear
2. Checkout reserves inventory, 15-min timeout - clear
3. Max one active promotion per product - clear
4. Discount codes vs promotional pricing are mutually exclusive - clear
5. Orders immutable after payment confirmed - clear
6. Zero inventory = cannot purchase - clear
7. Refunds within 30 days of delivery - clear

No contradictions. No real ambiguity. The spec is reasonably complete for what it states. 

There aren't any significant gaps that would require escalation, so I'll move forward with generating the JSON output.
